funnel.judge_summary: Tight upstream pass: vetoed patch-release noise, kept source-backed verdicts, and preserved conservative risk calls.

verdicts.what: Anthropic's official repo of reusable Skill bundles for AI coding agents.
verdicts.why_this_week: A new batch expanded the catalogue beyond the first few coding workflows.
verdicts.why_it_matters: If you already work inside Claude Code, Codex, or Cursor, reusable skill bundles turn repeat prompts into durable operating procedure.
verdicts.adoption_cost: ~10 min to inspect one skill and test it in a non-production repo. Low risk.
verdicts.next_action: Run a local lab on one skill, then install only the workflow you would actually use this week.

verdicts.what: Reference MCP servers that connect AI agents to databases, filesystems, browser tools, and developer APIs.
verdicts.why_this_week: MCP adoption is accelerating across coding agents, and reference servers are becoming the default integration path.
verdicts.why_it_matters: Teams adopting AI coding agents need tool access that is explicit, auditable, and easy to revoke. MCP is becoming that contract.
verdicts.adoption_cost: ~30 min to test one read-only server with throwaway credentials. Medium risk until permissions are reviewed.
verdicts.next_action: Trial one read-only MCP server against a sandbox project and document the permission boundary.

verdicts.what: Python framework for browser-driving agents powered by Playwright and LLM tool calls.
verdicts.why_this_week: Recent structured-output support makes browser tasks easier to evaluate and retry.
verdicts.why_it_matters: Research, QA, and competitive-intel workflows often need real browser interaction. This can replace brittle one-off scripts when tested carefully.
verdicts.adoption_cost: ~45 min to lab-test on a public site. Medium risk because browser automation is dependency-heavy.
verdicts.next_action: Lab-test one public browsing workflow and compare success rate, cost, and maintenance against a plain Playwright script.

verdicts.what: Open-weight coding model aimed at local or self-hosted code generation workflows.
verdicts.why_this_week: Fresh model release with enough community attention to watch independent evals closely.
verdicts.why_it_matters: Hosted frontier models still win for many teams, but local coding models can matter for privacy, latency, and fallback paths.
verdicts.adoption_cost: ~2 hrs to benchmark if you already have GPU capacity; otherwise not worth the infrastructure work yet.
verdicts.next_action: Monitor independent coding-agent evals for 30 days before spending integration time.

verdicts.what: Large open-weight reasoning model with a footprint beyond a normal laptop or small CI runner.
verdicts.why_this_week: High-visibility release, but size and operational requirements make it a poor first trial for most teams.
verdicts.why_it_matters: It is important ecosystem signal, not an immediate adoption candidate for a lean engineering team.
verdicts.adoption_cost: Weekend-scale GPU setup plus ongoing compute. High risk unless a real self-hosting need exists.
verdicts.next_action: Hold until smaller quantized variants and credible independent evals are available.
